summary: Iteration 70: All 20 tests PASSED. Verified (1) branch-specific suppliers with branch_id filtering, (2) product vendor linking via supplier_id with auto-fill of name/contact, (3) branch-specific moving average calculations in capital-preview and _apply_po_inventory, (4) smart capital rule: price >= current → last_purchase, price < current → moving_average.

FEATURE_1_BRANCH_SPECIFIC_SUPPLIERS.description: Suppliers are now scoped to branches via branch_id field
tests: test_01: POST /api/suppliers with branch_id creates branch-scoped supplier
test_02: Create supplier for different branch
test_04: GET /api/suppliers?branch_id=xxx returns branch + global suppliers
test_05: GET /api/suppliers without filter returns all
implementation: suppliers.py line 14-28: list_suppliers accepts Optional[str] branch_id, uses $or to include branch + global
suppliers.py line 31-51: create_supplier extracts branch_id from data
suppliers.py line 54-80: search_suppliers filters with $and/$or for branch filtering

FEATURE_2_VENDOR_SUPPLIER_LINKING.description: Product vendors can link to suppliers via supplier_id, auto-filling name/contact
tests: test_09: POST /api/products/{id}/vendors with supplier_id auto-fills name/contact from supplier
test_10: GET /api/products/{id}/detail enriches vendors with supplier details
implementation: products.py line 838-847: add_product_vendor looks up supplier by supplier_id and auto-fills vendor_name/vendor_contact
products.py line 535-545: get_product_detail enriches vendors with supplier details (email, address)

FEATURE_3_BRANCH_SPECIFIC_MOVING_AVERAGE.description: Moving average calculations are now branch-specific, not cross-branch
tests: test_14: Code review confirms branch filtering in _apply_po_inventory
test_19: Code review confirms capital-preview uses branch filter
implementation: purchase_orders.py line 60-68: _apply_po_inventory filters movements by branch_id for MA calculation
purchase_orders.py line 899-907: capital-preview filters purchase movements by po_branch_id

FEATURE_4_SMART_CAPITAL_RULE.description: Smart pricing rule: price >= current → last_purchase, price < current → moving_average (warns user)
tests: test_13: Smart capital rule price drop detection
test_20: Code review confirms smart rule implementation
implementation: purchase_orders.py line 83-93: Smart rule auto-selects capital method
If no explicit choice: price >= old_capital → use last_purchase, price < old_capital → use moving_average

critical_code_review_comments: All 4 features correctly implemented as requested
Supplier branch filtering uses proper $or query to include branch-specific + global suppliers
Vendor auto-fill from supplier works correctly - supplier_id lookup populates name/contact
Moving average calculation properly filters by branch_id in both _apply_po_inventory and capital-preview
Smart capital rule correctly defaults to moving_average when new price drops below current capital
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed suppliers and products during test execution, cleaned up after tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 70 verified all 4 bug fix/feature implementations: (1) Suppliers now support branch_id field with proper filtering via $or query, (2) Product vendors can link to suppliers via supplier_id with auto-fill of name/contact from supplier record, (3) Moving average calculations in capital-preview and _apply_po_inventory are now branch-specific using branch_id filter, (4) Smart capital rule auto-selects: price >= current → last_purchase, price < current → moving_average. Database has existing production data. All tests passed.
